svg.text: FRCS Part 1 (General Surgery) · UK practice (NICE · BSG) · diagnosis · LA grade · Barrett’s · treatment
Reflux → mucosal injury → intestinal metaplasia → dysplasia (low- then high-grade) → oesophageal adenocarcinoma — the metaplasia–dysplasia–carcinoma sequence.
heartburn & acid regurgitation — worse lying, bending
dysphagia; age ≥55 with weight loss + upper-abdo pain
/ reflux / dyspepsia; GI bleeding; anaemia; vomiting;
ambulatory study OFF PPI — gold standard; acid exposure
LA-B or worse = objective GORD; LA-A or normal OGD →
≤5 mm mucosal break(s), not extending between the tops of two mucosal folds (borderline — confirm GORD with pH)
>5 mm mucosal break(s), not continuous between the tops of two folds (LA-B or worse = objective GORD)
break(s) continuous between the tops of ≥2 folds, but involving <75% of the circumference
mucosal break(s) involving ≥75% of the oesophageal circumference (most severe)
distal oesophageal squamous epithelium replaced by columnar epithelium
endoscopically visible columnar-lined oesophagus ≥1 cm above the GOJ, biopsy-confirmed;
intestinal metaplasia (goblet cells) often present & raises risk but is NOT required for the
Prague C&M — C = circumferential, M = maximal extent (cm above GOJ); biopsy by Seattle
progression to oesophageal adenocarcinoma ~0.3% / year (non-dysplastic); higher with
weight loss, raise head of bed, smaller & earlier meals,
avoid trigger foods, stop smoking, cut alcohol
antacid; treat H. pylori if present
refractory symptoms, volume regurgitation or patient
Toupet 270°, Dor) ± hiatus hernia repair; LINX magnetic
high-dose PPI ± aspirin cut HGD / adenocarcinoma /
EMR / ESD of visible lesions + RFA of residual flat
recurrence is common after ablation — ongoing
Dysphagia, weight loss, GI bleeding or anaemia with reflux → urgent (2ww) OGD — never just start a PPI.
Diagnose GORD objectively: LA-B+ oesophagitis or a positive pH study — a PPI response alone is not proof.
Do pH studies OFF PPI; manometry is mandatory before fundoplication (a missed achalasia is a disaster).
Barrett’s is a histological diagnosis — measure with Prague C&M and biopsy by the Seattle protocol.
Do NOT ablate non-dysplastic Barrett’s — surveil it; reserve EMR / RFA for confirmed dysplasia or IMC.
Low-grade dysplasia must be confirmed by two GI pathologists before offering RFA.
Sources: NICE NG12 (suspected cancer referral) · NICE CG184 (dyspepsia & GORD) · NICE NG231 (Barrett’s & stage 1 oesophageal adenocarcinoma, 2023) · BSG Barrett’s guidelines
Key trial: AspECT (Jankowski et al., Lancet 2018) — high-dose esomeprazole ± aspirin in Barrett’s. LA classification: Lundell et al., Gut 1999. IMC = intramucosal carcinoma.
Revision aid — verify against current local guidance before clinical use.
Revision resources & realistic mock exams · mockfrcs.co.uk
